articles.title: Bringing automated preview, review, and merge to Claude Code on desktop
articles.why: Single tool for preview, review, merge—no context switching
articles.description: Claude Code desktop now previews running apps, auto-reviews code diffs with inline comments, monitors PR status, and can auto-fix CI failures and merge PRs—eliminating the need to juggle multiple windows

articles.title: 2026 Trader Bags: Are We Really Betting on This Mix of Coins?
articles.why: Distinguish hype from substance when evaluating tech
articles.description: Portfolio curator questions whether the 7 most-voted cryptocurrencies represent sound technology or speculative hype—revealing that the list was built via polls rather than rigorous on-chain metrics

articles.title: Scaling Headless Browsers: Managing Contexts vs. Instances
articles.why: Contexts are 50-150MB cheaper; scale hundreds per browser
articles.description: Instances are heavyweight (separate GPU/network per session), contexts are lightweight (share one browser). One browser hosts hundreds of contexts—trade-off: shared process means all contexts fail if browser crashes

articles.title: The third era of AI software development
articles.why: Autonomous agents handle tasks over hours/days independently
articles.description: Era 1: tab autocomplete. Era 2: synchronous agents via back-and-forth prompts. Era 3: cloud agents working asynchronously on longer tasks, reviewed via logs/video rather than diffs—one-third of Cursor PRs now from agents

articles.title: Code Mode: give agents an entire API in 1,000 tokens
articles.why: 99.9% token reduction for API access—agents explore APIs dynamically
articles.description: Instead of listing all API endpoints as tools, agents use search() and execute() to explore and call APIs dynamically. Cloudflare's 2,500 endpoints fit in ~1,000 tokens instead of 1.17M

articles.title: Measuring actual AI Impact for Engineering with Apache DevLake
articles.why: Correlate AI adoption to real metrics, not vanity numbers
articles.description: Apache DevLake normalizes 20+ DevOps tools into one queryable database, revealing correlations like '75%+ Copilot adoption = 33% faster PR cycles and 2x deployment frequency'

articles.title: Building frontend UIs with Codex and Figma
articles.description: Figma MCP integration enables design-to-code generation and code-to-design capture, allowing teams to iterate between exploration and execution without manual recreation

videos.title: When open-sourcing your code goes wrong...
videos.why: Public code reflects on your project; broken code damages credibility
videos.description: Open-sourcing requires proper packaging, testing, and documentation. Broken or unmaintainable code creates negative impression that extends beyond the code itself

videos.title: Only extreme programmers will survive
videos.why: Extreme Programming disciplines—TDD, pair programming, refactoring—separate elite developers
videos.description: Extreme Programming demands simplicity, communication, feedback, courage, and respect. Pair programming, test-driven development, and relentless refactoring are core practices that most developers lack

videos.title: 4 Skills I'm Learning that AI Can't Replace (backed by data)
videos.why: Build resilience against AI automation; maintain competitive advantage
videos.description: The Cockpit Rule (when to delegate vs. collaborate with AI), narrative/storytelling to move people, strategic communication frameworks, and intentional cognitive independence to avoid atrophy

videos.title: Linux Commands in 30 Mins (Real-World Example)
videos.why: Linux proficiency remains essential; command-line skills never go obsolete
videos.description: Real-world Linux commands and workflows—core fundamentals for any developer working in production environments

videos.title: Delete your CLAUDE.md (and your AGENT.md too)
videos.why: Long CLAUDE.md files lose impact; Claude ignores half the content in noise
videos.description: Keep CLAUDE.md short and focused—ruthlessly prune unnecessary rules so critical guardrails don't get buried. Overly verbose files dilute the signal

videos.title: A computer for every agent
videos.why: Agents operating in sandboxed environments is the future of AI work
videos.description: Computer use is key to true agent autonomy—sandboxed, isolated computers let agents interact with UIs, navigate apps, and complete full workflows independently

videos.title: Cursor now shows you demos, not diffs
videos.why: Video demonstrations show what code does, not just what changed
videos.description: Agents send videos of their work instead of raw diffs—visual proof of functionality beats line-by-line code reviews for agent output

videos.title: OpenClaw Creator Peter Steinberger's advice for developers
videos.why: Insights from building $100M dev tool; how to work effectively with AI agents
videos.description: Be playful, embrace iterative improvement like learning guitar. Your role shifts to architect, editor, prompt engineer—ship code you haven't read; use 5-10 agents in parallel

podcasts.title: Episode 501: Vibecoding CEO and doing to teaching
podcasts.why: Understand pivot from teaching coding to building agents that code
podcasts.description: Amjad Masad (Replit CEO) on his controversial shift: from teaching millions to code to building AI agents that do it for you. Vibe coding democratizes software creation beyond technical experts

podcasts.title: The Career Bet Every Engineer Must Make
podcasts.why: Understand career implications of AI—traditional developer roles are ending
podcasts.description: Former Meta I9 engineer Philip Su explains why writing code is no longer your most valuable skill. AI democratizes creation; design, product thinking, and taste become the real constraints

podcasts.title: The mythical agent-month (News)
podcasts.why: Learn what AI agents can't solve—Brooks's Law still applies
podcasts.description: Design, product scoping, and taste remain practical constraints on quality software, even with AI agents. Adding agents doesn't solve fundamental estimation and scope problems

podcasts.title: Mitchell Hashimoto's new way of writing code
podcasts.why: Learn how elite developers work with parallel AI agents
podcasts.description: Always run agents asynchronously in background—while you code, agent plans; while it codes, you review. Kick off tasks before leaving so work progresses continuously
